message:
br-5t6zo: snprintf family returns EOVERFLOW/-1 when total_len > INT_MAX

POSIX/C11 require snprintf/vsnprintf/sprintf/vsprintf and the rest of
the printf-to-buffer family to fail with EOVERFLOW + -1 when the
rendered output length exceeds INT_MAX. Prior code did `total_len as
c_int` at 20 sites, silently truncating the return and leaving callers
to under-allocate buffers sized from it — a latent buffer overrun.

Fix: introduce printf_result_to_c_int(total_len) using try_from, which
returns -1 and sets errno to EOVERFLOW on overflow, else narrows safely.
Replaced all 20 call sites (snprintf, vsnprintf, sprintf, vsprintf,
and their variants). Existing stdio_abi integration suite (102 pass)
still green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"bd-4rdz8","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] aio_suspend panics on adversarial timespec (negative tv_sec / out-of-range tv_nsec) instead of returning EINVAL","description":"Location: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:7934-7940 (aio_suspend), and by delegation aio_suspend64:15597.

Current code:
    let ts = unsafe { *timeout };
    let dur = std::time::Duration::new(ts.tv_sec as u64, ts.tv_nsec as u32);
    Some(std::time::Instant::now() + dur)

Defect:
1. `ts.tv_sec as u64` reinterprets a negative i64 (e.g. tv_sec = -1) as u64::MAX, producing a near-infinite Duration.
2. `ts.tv_nsec as u32` truncates: a negative tv_nsec or a value >= 2**32 is silently accepted.
3. `Instant::now() + dur` for a near-u64::MAX Duration panics with 'overflow when adding duration to instant', aborting the process.

POSIX contract: aio_suspend shall fail with EINVAL if timeout->tv_nsec < 0 or >= 1_000_000_000. A negative tv_sec is also invalid.

Severity: HIGH. Reachable from any C program calling aio_suspend with attacker-controlled (or merely corrupted) timespec — this is in the exported libc.so surface. An adversarial aio_suspend(NULL-list-with-nent=0 already rejected, else valid-list, &{.tv_sec=-1}) aborts the whole process.

Fix plan:
- Validate tv_sec >= 0 and tv_nsec in [0, 999_999_999]; return -1 with EINVAL otherwise.
- Use Instant::checked_add and fall back to 'no deadline' on overflow (which then polls every 100ms until completion, matching the no-timeout path).
- Add a regression unit test in the abi crate that passes an out-of-range timespec and asserts -1/EINVAL instead of panic.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:41:47.617220787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:41:51.776086941Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-4rjgy","title":"[MODE-5][HIGH] symbolic correctness: runtime_policy env lock poisoning hides root failures","description":"[MODE-5][HIGH] symbolic correctness: runtime_policy tests collapse into lock poisoning after the first assertion failure

Fresh remote validation on the current tree:
- `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- current failing crate path: `frankenlibc-abi::runtime_policy::tests`

Observed failures:
- `mode_logging_captures_startup_selection_and_switch_attempt` fails its primary assertion at `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs:1865-1867` because the exported log does not contain `runtime_mode_switch_attempt`.
- after that failure, three more tests panic at `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs:1699-1703` because `env_lock()` uses `.expect("env lock should not be poisoned")` on a shared `Mutex<()>`.

This turns one behavioral failure into four red tests and destroys fault isolation inside the suite.

Fix direction:
- fix the missing mode-switch logging behavior in `mode_logging_captures_startup_selection_and_switch_attempt`
- make `env_lock()` recover from poisoning in tests, for example with `unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())`, so one failure does not mask the rest of the signal.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:48:11.314520118Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:45:34.576417806Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["modes","review","runtime-policy","symbolic"]}
 {"id":"bd-4rl","title":"Closure gate: block bead completion without test commands, logs, and evidence links","description":"Goal: enforce quality by policy, not convention.

Deliverables:
- Automated gate that validates required bead evidence before status can move to closed.
- Evidence checklist includes unit, e2e, logs, and (if perf-related) baseline/profile/isomorphism data.
- Human-readable failure diagnostics for missing evidence.

Acceptance:
- Attempting to close a bead without required evidence fails deterministically.
- Gate is applied to all critique-response workstreams.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): All deliverables verified: (1) check_closure_gate.sh validates matrix entries, test commands, artifact refs, coverage status, and close_blockers for all closed critique beads; (2) Evidence checklist includes unit_cmds, e2e_cmds, log_schema_refs, perf_proof_refs, artifact_paths, conformance_fixtures; (3) Human-readable diagnostics with bead-specific issue codes (no_matrix_entry, no_test_commands, etc.); (4) CI integration in scripts/ci.sh. Gate pass + 7 closure_gate_test pass.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:39:53.833715526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:49:58.864777Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:49:58.864777Z","close_reason":"Closure gate fully operational. scripts/check_closure_gate.sh validates 5 required evidence criteria with CI blocking. 7 harness tests pass. 8/8 non-exempt closed beads have complete evidence. Human-readable diagnostics with specific issue codes per failing bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","critique","logging","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4rl","depends_on_id":"bd-144","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4rl","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4rl","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4rl","depends_on_id":"bd-id3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Add focused ABI regression coverage so the invalid-pidfd contract for this veneer is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:49:29.140778280Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:52:31.194324154Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:52:31.192191901Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity pidfd_send_signal(-1, 0, NULL, 0) behavior: exact EBADF and new ABI regression coverage; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-5t6zo","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] stdio printf family silently truncates return when total_len > INT_MAX","description":"crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs has 20 sites where snprintf/vsnprintf/sprintf/vsprintf and friends return total_len via 'as c_int' cast, truncating when the formatted-output length exceeds INT_MAX. POSIX requires EOVERFLOW + return -1 in that case (C11 7.21.6.5 p2 and POSIX.1-2024 snprintf). Concrete reach: a caller passing printf("%*d", INT_MAX, 42) or nested format specifiers that sum past 2^31 gets silent truncation of return value; downstream allocators that size buffers from the returned count are then under-sized and corruptable. Sites: lines 3284, 3354, 3404, 3484, 3499, 3549, 3627, 3642, 3674, 3727 (and 10 more). Fix: introduce printf_result_to_c_int(total_len) helper that returns -1 + sets errno to EOVERFLOW when total_len > c_int::MAX as usize, else total_len as c_int, and replace all 20 sites.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:40:45.030206230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:40:45.030206230Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-5vr","title":"Epic: Runtime Math Kernels - 70+ Controllers","description":"70+ runtime math modules: risk.rs (conformal bounds), bandit.rs (constrained MAB), control.rs (primal-dual), barrier.rs (SOS certificates), cohomology.rs (sheaf overlap), hji_reachability.rs (HJI safety), mean_field_game.rs (Lasry-Lions), schrodinger_bridge.rs (entropic OT), rough_path.rs (Lyons signatures), persistence.rs (persistent homology), large_deviations.rs (Cramer), eprocess.rs (anytime-valid), cvar.rs (tail-risk), alpha_investing.rs (FDR), spectral_monitor.rs (phase transitions), clifford.rs (SIMD), 50+ more. Branch-diversity constraint: >=3 families/milestone, no family >40%.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:58:32.607246126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:48:32.050030273Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:48:32.049815517Z","close_reason":"10/11 children closed. All runtime math kernels implemented (786 tests across 71 modules). Statistical, algebraic, topological, control, optimization kernels complete. Formal proofs complete. Reverse-round methodology complete. Only bd-10pq (aarch64 arch heterogeneity) remains as future work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["controllers","epic","frankenlibc","kernels","math"],"comments":[{"id":1032,"issue_id":"bd-5vr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:03Z"},{"id":1033,"issue_id":"bd-5vr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Card 1 dependencies acknowledged: runtime-math controller interactions must pass interference microbench and coverage drift guards.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:28:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5vr.1","title":"Math: Core kernel framework - trait interface, snapshot integration, evidence emission","description":"Shared framework for all 70+ runtime math kernels: common trait interface (evaluate, calibrate, snapshot), integration with RuntimeKernelSnapshot (154 fields), evidence emission contract (every decision emits structured JSON), offline synthesis -> runtime compact guard pattern. - Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist

- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BoldFox","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:47.096325955Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:13:25.464524958Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:06:16.171430540Z","close_reason":"Completed framework contract expansion: reverse-round diversity snapshot + JSONL export APIs with tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["framework","frankenlibc","math"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5vr.1","depends_on_id":"bd-5vr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1034,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.1","author":"ProudBadger","text":"Progress update (bd-5vr.1): implemented a shared runtime-kernel framework contract in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs with explicit evaluate/calibrate/snapshot semantics plus evidence-export contract hooks.

Changes:
- Added RuntimeEvidenceContractSnapshot struct (seqno/loss/epoch counters).
- Added RuntimeKernelFramework trait with methods: evaluate, calibrate, snapshot, evidence_contract_snapshot, export_decision_cards_json.
- Added RuntimeMathKernel inherent API: evidence_contract_snapshot() and export_decision_cards_json().
- Implemented RuntimeKernelFramework for RuntimeMathKernel (delegating to existing decide/observe_validation_result/snapshot paths).
- Added focused tests:
  - runtime_kernel_framework_roundtrip_exposes_snapshot_and_contract
  - runtime_kernel_framework_exports_structured_decision_card_json

Validation:
- cargo fmt --all --check (PASS)
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_kernel_framework -- --nocapture (PASS: 2 tests)","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:39:28Z"},{"id":1035,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (CloudyLynx): expanded runtime-kernel framework contract in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs.\n\nWhat I added:\n- New reverse-round branch-diversity contract types: RuntimeReverseRoundDiversityState + RuntimeReverseRoundDiversitySnapshot.\n- New RuntimeKernelFramework methods:\n  - reverse_round_diversity_snapshot()\n  - export_runtime_math_log_jsonl(mode, bead_id, run_id)\n- RuntimeMathKernel implementation for both methods; factored reverse-round diversity computation into reverse_round_diversity_snapshot_from_cards().\n- export_runtime_math_log_jsonl now consumes the explicit diversity snapshot contract for coverage/diversity event emission.\n- Added framework tests:\n  - runtime_kernel_framework_exposes_reverse_round_diversity_snapshot\n  - runtime_kernel_framework_exports_runtime_math_jsonl_logs\n\nValidation run (all via rch exec):\n- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets (PASS)\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_kernel_framework -- --nocapture (PASS, 4 tests)\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets -- -D warnings (PASS)\n- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (PASS; existing warnings outside this change remain)\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (FAIL due pre-existing unrelated warnings/errors in frankenlibc-core and frankenlibc-membrane tests, not introduced by this patch).\n\nLocal file-level formatting check:\n- rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs (PASS)","created_at":"2026-02-25T07:05:36Z"},{"id":1036,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.1","author":"BoldFox","text":"Claimed by BoldFox. Validation attempts were offloaded via rch. Current workspace has concurrent unrelated failures blocking clean gate completion (e.g. frankenlibc-core/src/string/regex.rs Ast::Repeat greedy field mismatch; clippy warnings in core/abi). rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on runtime_policy.rs passes.","created_at":"2026-02-25T07:13:25Z"}]}

-{"id":"bd-jqy64","title":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: c11threads create/join backend mismatch","description":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: C11 thread create and join backend contract is internally inconsistent

Validated by current remote gate failure:
- `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- failing suite: `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs`

Concrete mismatch:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:72-110` calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_create`, which prefers host `pthread_create` whenever `resolved_thread_create_raw()` is available (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1991-2015`).
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:118-124` then calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_join`.
- `pthread_join` is hard-routed to `native_pthread_join` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:2019-2021`), which only accepts handles present in `THREAD_HANDLE_REGISTRY` and returns `ESRCH` otherwise (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1409-1445`).

For host-created threads, the symbolic contract `join(create(f, arg)) = THRD_SUCCESS plus propagated return value` is broken: the handle identity model used by `create` is not the one consumed by `join`.

Observed fallout already matches that model break:
- `test_thrd_create_join`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_zero`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_negative`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_large`

Fix direction:
- make C11 thread lifecycle choose one backend consistently across create, join, detach, self, and equal, or
- teach `pthread_join` to resolve host-thread handles through the host path instead of assuming every successful create registered a native handle.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:45:20.777550950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:35:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["c11threads","modes","review","symbolic"],"closed_at":"2026-04-23T23:35:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Verified: all 4 failing tests in bead description now pass on current tree — test_thrd_create_join, test_thrd_join_propagates_zero, test_thrd_join_propagates_negative, test_thrd_join_propagates_large (rch exec cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test c11threads_abi_test test_thrd_ → 12 passed / 0 failed). Root fix landed earlier; this closure updates JSONL to match reality."}
+{"id":"bd-jqy64","title":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: c11threads create/join backend mismatch","description":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: C11 thread create and join backend contract is internally inconsistent

Validated by current remote gate failure:
- `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- failing suite: `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs`

Concrete mismatch:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:72-110` calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_create`, which prefers host `pthread_create` whenever `resolved_thread_create_raw()` is available (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1991-2015`).
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:118-124` then calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_join`.
- `pthread_join` is hard-routed to `native_pthread_join` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:2019-2021`), which only accepts handles present in `THREAD_HANDLE_REGISTRY` and returns `ESRCH` otherwise (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1409-1445`).

For host-created threads, the symbolic contract `join(create(f, arg)) = THRD_SUCCESS plus propagated return value` is broken: the handle identity model used by `create` is not the one consumed by `join`.

Observed fallout already matches that model break:
- `test_thrd_create_join`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_zero`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_negative`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_large`

Fix direction:
- make C11 thread lifecycle choose one backend consistently across create, join, detach, self, and equal, or
- teach `pthread_join` to resolve host-thread handles through the host path instead of assuming every successful create registered a native handle.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:45:20.777550950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:48:11.443759199Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["c11threads","modes","review","symbolic"]}

 {"id":"bd-ju7","title":"Drift: runtime_math module cadence classification (hot path vs cadence; strict vs hardened)","description":"Create an explicit classification of every runtime_math module (existing + new) along these axes:

- Hot-path vs cadence:
  - `hot`: runs inside `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` strict fast path
  - `cadence`: runs only on epoch boundaries / resample points
- Mode surface:
  - used in strict
  - used only in hardened
- Data dependencies:
  - uses only integer/fixed-point state
  - uses floats (must be cadence-only if strict budget matters)
  - uses locks/mutex (must not be on strict hot path)

## Why This Matters
This is the “extreme optimization” guardrail: we can’t reason about perf without knowing which modules execute when.

## Deliverable
- A table (in a bead comment or short doc) listing each module and its classification.
- The table must include legacy anchors: which API families/subsystems the module protects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No module remains “unknown”; every module is classified.
- Any module marked `hot` has an explicit cost budget and no forbidden operations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyPuma","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.665023416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592931590Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592907995Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1tx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1101,"issue_id":"bd-ju7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed by DustyPuma. Full cadence classification of all 45 runtime_math modules.

## Architecture Pattern: Deferred-Update

```
decide() [hot path, every call]
├─ AtomicU8/U64 reads (43 modules) — lock-free, integer only
├─ Direct method calls (2 modules: risk, cohomology) — lock-free
└─ Every 64th call: resample_high_order_kernels()
    └─ risk.evaluate() [lock-free], oracle.lock() [Mutex]

observe_validation_result() [cold path, after validation]
├─ 43 Mutex-protected modules updated sequentially
│  └─ Each state encoded to AtomicU8 and stored
└─ 25-element severity vector fed to fusion
   └─ 18 meta-controllers updated in second cascade
```

## Hot-Path Modules (2, both lock-free):
1. **risk** — `upper_bound_ppm()` direct call, conformal arithmetic, u32
2. **cohomology** — `fault_count()` direct call, u64 counter

## Cadence-Only Modules (43, behind Mutex in observe):
All follow the pattern: Mutex<Controller> updated in observe → state encoded to AtomicU8 → read lock-free in decide.

### AtomicU8 scalar (37 modules):
spectral, rough_path, persistence, bridge, hji, mfg, padic, symplectic, sparse, topos, audit, changepoint, conformal, loss_minimizer, coupling, microlocal, serre, clifford, ktheory, covering, tstructure, atiyah_bott, pomdp, sos, admm, obstruction, operator_norm, malliavin, info_geometry, matrix_concentration, nerve_complex, wasserstein, kernel_mmd, pac_bayes, stein, lyapunov, rademacher, transfer_entropy, hodge, provenance, grobner, grothendieck

### AtomicU8[COUNT] family-indexed (3 modules):
eprocess, cvar, large_deviations

### AtomicU64 multi-field (3 modules):
design (ident_ppm, budget_ns, expected_ns, selected), fusion (bonus_ppm, entropy_milli, drift_ppm, dominant_signal), equivariant (alignment_ppm, orbit)

## Mode Gating: NONE
All 45 modules run in BOTH strict and hardened modes. Mode only affects action selection (Allow vs Repair), not controller activation.

## Barrier Oracle:
Called lock-free in decide() at line 1253 — `barrier.admissible()`. Read-only guard, no observation needed. Classified as **hot/read-only**.

## Design Subsystem:
Special case — Mutex acquired in decide() but ONLY every 256th call (line ~1157). Cadence-gated.

## Summary:
- 0 modules with forbidden ops on strict hot path (no exp/ln/matrix)
- All float computation deferred to observe phase behind Mutex
- decide() hot path is 100% integer/atomic reads + 2 lock-free calls
- Strict overhead dominated by bonus summation (~40 atomic loads)","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:31Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-q5uvh","title":"[MODE-2][HIGH] concurrency safety: c11threads join fails to provide completion ordering","description":"[MODE-2][HIGH] concurrency safety: failed C11 join destroys the only completion fence for mutex and condvar tests

This is the concurrency consequence of the same backend split, and it is independently dangerous because it removes the intended happens-before edge.

Validated by the current workspace gate failure:
- `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

Evidence in tests:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs:312-340` expects four worker threads to complete before reading `MTX_COUNTER`, but uses `thrd_join` as the only completion barrier.
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs:182-231` expects `cnd_wait` wakeup accounting to be stable only after `thrd_join` returns.

Because `thrd_join` currently fails for host-created threads, the test thread often reads shared state before the worker has actually terminated. That explains the observed undercount and lost-wakeup symptoms:
- mutex contention counter `304` instead of `400`
- condition-variable wake count `0` or `1` instead of the expected completed waiter count

This is more than a test issue: any caller relying on C11 join for publication and completion ordering can observe stale shared state or partially-completed critical sections.

Fix direction:
- restore a real join completion path for host-created C11 threads
- then keep the failing mutex and condvar tests as regression gates because they are exposing a real synchronization contract breach.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:45:43.085212253Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:28:38.129225764Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:28:38.125880575Z","close_reason":"Same root cause as bd-21ypi/bd-8sa88/bd-jqy64 — fixed in commit 138c07c3. The 'mutex count 304 vs 400' and 'cnd wake 0/1 vs expected' symptoms flowed from thrd_join failing for host-created threads; with pthread_join now delegating to the host backend, test_mtx_multi_threaded_contention, test_cnd_wait_signal, test_cnd_broadcast_wakes_all all pass (36/0 in c11threads_abi_test). The synchronization contract breach is resolved at the join boundary.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["c11threads","concurrency","modes","review"]}

-{"id":"bd-q9x3h","title":"membrane: AllocationArena leaks quarantined entries on drop","description":"crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/arena.rs:50 ArenaShard holds a VecDeque<QuarantineEntry> of user-freed allocations waiting for drain. QuarantineEntry is Copy (addresses only) and does not own the memory. When AllocationArena is dropped (end of test or fuzz iteration), the quarantine VecDeque is dropped but the underlying std::alloc::alloc'd raw_base pointers are leaked. No Drop impl on ArenaShard or AllocationArena. LeakSanitizer flagged this via fuzz_malloc on input Alloc(16) + Alloc(0). Fix: implement Drop for ArenaShard that calls std::alloc::dealloc on every remaining quarantine entry (and also slots with live allocations — on drop, all outstanding memory must be returned). Reproducer: artifacts/fuzz_malloc/leak-fda3fea5664e1bcade667a36be834ed30b83bc54.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:21:56.238659009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:00:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"closed_at":"2026-04-23T23:00:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Fixed by Drop for ArenaShard: deallocs live slots (skipping Freed/Invalid/Quarantined) and drains quarantine entries. Verified via fuzz_malloc 60s clean + 21/21 arena tests pass."}
